title: Outbound emails from a shared outlook mailbox
excerpt: I want to create an automation so that when a status changes an email is sent out FROM A SHARED outlook mailbox. We have multiple people approving requests coming into our Monday board and whoever changes the status the …

posts.author: neilpenry (Neil Penry)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to create an automation so that when a status changes an email is sent out FROM A SHARED outlook mailbox. We have multiple people approving requests coming into our Monday board and whoever changes the status the email should come from the “team outlook mailbox”. Firstly, is this possible and secondly I have had no success in using the outlook recipe as it says I need to “ask an admin to grant permission to this app before you can use it” - however I can’t find any documentation relating to what permissions I need to ask my admin to grant. Can anyone help with both of these questions?

posts.author: JamieVassalli (Jamie Vassalli)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I have had success with using a shared inbox for automations of this type. You will need to reach out to your admin to grant permission to use the outlook integration (I sent an email letting them know what I was using it for and needed access). You will also need to reach out to IT for the password to the shared inbox as the outlook integration requires a password to set it up.

posts.author: matts (Matt S)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JamieVassalli, thanks for sharing your experience and I’m glad you got it to work.

Unfortunately, I’m trying to do the same thing, but with a Shared Mailbox which is a special type of account that doesn’t have a password (users must be delegate access by an admin). I believe this is what @neilpenry was asking about, too.

posts.author: osamahaskari (Osamah Askari)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @matts I totally get what you’re struggling with, I have created a Monday App to overcome this, its called SharedMailer and it does exactly what you and I struggled with. its in Early Access right now so I wont show up in marketplace until I complete all the requirement to get app approved like the documentation and everything. But I’m keeping this monday.com app free to use in its early life and I might then make it $2 monthly or $12 yearly. But let me know if you’re still in need and I can give you direct access to the application and short video of how to use it.

image2483×1234 180 KB

posts.author: matts (Matt S)
posts.cookedHtml: Awesome stuff! This currently isn’t on our roadmap, so we’ll hold off for now. Will keep you and your project in mind. Thanks!
